title: Grimdark mud calculus
text: Every choice spends something permanent; hope is a budget line, not a theme song.

title: Gothic rain-soaked ruin
text: Cracked manors, family curses, and manners sharper than blades under constant rain.

title: Cosmic indifferent sky
text: Stars that do not care; knowledge scrapes sanity; survival is small and temporary.

title: Folk harvest secrets
text: Village smiles, harvest rites, and outsiders who leave wrong or stay wronger.

title: Trench grit campaign
text: Mud, ration rot, and officers who measure lives in meters of ground.

title: Body-clinic black market
text: Medicine that rewrites flesh; clinics sell upgrades with side effects that have names.

title: Widowlight manor
text: A manor where clocks disagree and servants polish silver that never stays clean.
examples: dining room set for twelve ghosts

title: Plague-barred quarter
text: Gates painted with marks; healers bargain for gloves and names of the dead.

title: Quiet wing of the asylum
text: A wing where patients write true maps on walls; staff call it art.

title: Bone-buttress cathedral
text: A cathedral built of victory bones; sermons double as recruitment.

title: Fen of false lights
text: Marsh lights that look like rescue; boats rot faster than maps update.

title: Trench-town of dugouts
text: A town of dugouts and ration queues where the front is always one hill over.

title: Ash-seal inquisitors
text: An order that burns heresy and sometimes the evidence of better futures.

title: Corpse-cart guild
text: They haul the dead and sell what the dead no longer need, quietly.

title: Circle of the Wrong Stars
text: A cult that charts stars that should not rise; membership is a map you regret reading.

title: Black clinic cooperative
text: Surgeons without licenses who price mercy in organs and secrets.

title: Scar-itch omen
text: Old scars itch before betrayal or weather disaster; often wrong, sometimes life-saving.

title: Funeral cold-reading
text: Guess one true practical need of the recently dead from the room, not their secrets.
examples: they needed the key, not revenge

title: Shadow-slip half-step
text: Step into a shadow for a blink; bright rooms and true names yank you back hard.

title: Blood-tally memory
text: Remember who bled where in a room; starts as stains only you notice.

title: Sanity ledger tick
text: Track your own fraying as numbers only you see; useful until the numbers lie.

title: Bleak but fair pressure
text: The world is cruel without random spite; smart play still helps, soft hearts still bruise.

title: Slow-building dread
text: Fear accumulates in small wrong details; jumpscare is rare, unease is constant.

title: Scar economy death rules
text: Death is rare; maiming is common. Scars change rolls and social doors.

title: Rain-soaked funeral hire
text: Hired as a spare pallbearer; the corpse is lighter than it should be.

title: Deserter papers in pocket
text: Wake with another soldier's papers; the war thinks you are still owed to it.

title: Cult invite misdelivered
text: An invite to a private sky-viewing arrives with your name almost right.
